description: Colombian drug trafficking organization based in Cali, founded in the 1970s by the Rodríguez Orejuela brothers, José Santacruz Londoño, and Hélmer Herrera Buitrago. It was a major cocaine trafficking and money laundering enterprise, known for its political and economic connections. The cartel was dismantled in the late 1990s after its leaders were captured or killed.

country_links.evidence_quote: importando pasta de coca desde Bolivia y Perú
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel imported coca paste from Bolivia.

country_links.evidence_quote: un avión A-37 Dragonfly comprado en El Salvador
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel bought an A-37 Dragonfly aircraft in El Salvador for a bombing attempt.

country_links.evidence_quote: el cartel de Cali estableció una alianza estratégica con la poderosa organización criminal Camorra
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel established a strategic alliance with the Camorra, supplying cocaine for distribution in Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: Rodríguez manejó las finanzas hacia Estados Unidos, Europa y Japón
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel managed finances towards Japan.

country_links.evidence_quote: importando pasta de coca desde Bolivia y Perú
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel imported coca paste from Peru.

country_links.evidence_quote: el cartel comenzó a expandir sus actividades en Europa, desarrollando una relación de trabajo con los contrabandistas de tabaco de Galicia en España
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel expanded activities in Europe, developing a working relationship with tobacco smugglers in Galicia, Spain.

country_links.evidence_quote: Santacruz y la organización de Pacho Herrera exportaban cuatro de cada cinco gramos de cocaína que circulan en Nueva York
country_links.context: The Cali Cartel exported cocaine to the United States, with a significant portion circulating in New York.
